name: Representing disasters
description: The devastating Indian Ocean tsunami was captured by many people on personal devices and home cameras, with the footage broadcast to the world. The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

key_work.title: Phi Phi Island after the Indian Ocean tsunami
key_work.brief_description: How the media produces news changed dramatically when bystanders captured the 2004 Indian Ocean earthquake and tsunami on handheld cameras. After obtaining the footage, news outlets circulated it around the globe. Today, social media allows spectators to share footage and images of world events instantly.

constellations_primary.name: Representing disasters
constellations_primary.description: The devastating Indian Ocean tsunami was captured by many people on personal devices and home cameras, with the footage broadcast to the world. The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

constellations_other.name: Speculative futures, sci-fi worlds and environmental disaster
constellations_other.description: Deborah Kelly's The Gods of Tiny Things explores extinction and the climate crisis, the scars of colonialism, and the political shift to the right around the world through collage and imagined futures, much like these sci-fi, animated and computer-generated artworks.

start.title: The Perfect Storm
start.creator_credit: Wolfgang Petersen, Erica Edell Phillips, Gail Katz, Paula Weinstein, Baltimore Spring Creek Productions, Radiant Productions, Warner Bros. Pictures, William Sandell and William D. Wittliff
start.brief_description: In October 1991, a confluence of weather conditions combined to form a killer storm in the North Atlantic. Caught in the storm was the sword-fishing boat Andrea Gail. Magnificent foreshadowing and anticipation fill this true-life drama while minute details of the fishing boats, their gear and the weather are juxtaposed with the sea adventure.

constellations_other.name: Representing disasters
constellations_other.description: The devastating Indian Ocean tsunami was captured by many people on personal devices and home cameras, with the footage broadcast to the world. The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

start.creator_credit_for_label: Wolfgang Petersen, Erica Edell Phillips, Gail Katz, Paula Weinstein, Baltimore Spring Creek Productions, Radiant Productions, Warner Bros. Pictures, William Sandell and William D. Wittliff

end.title: Twister
end.creator_credit: Jan de Bont, Ellen Mirojnick, Ian Bryce, Kathleen Kennedy, Michael Crichton, Amblin Entertainment, Constant c Productions, Twister Productions, Universal Pictures, Warner Bros. Pictures, Joseph C. Nemec III, Anne-Marie Martin and Ken Nielsen
end.brief_description: TV weatherman Bill Harding is trying to get his tornado-hunter wife, Jo, to sign divorce papers so he can marry his girlfriend Melissa. But Mother Nature, in the form of a series of intense storms sweeping across Oklahoma, has other plans.

constellations_other.name: Representing disasters
constellations_other.description: The devastating Indian Ocean tsunami was captured by many people on personal devices and home cameras, with the footage broadcast to the world. The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

constellations_other.name: Make it cute, make them think
constellations_other.description: Cartoons and anime are known for their dynamic and vibrant visuals, but beneath the cuteness they can also deliver powerful environmental messages and address the climate crisis.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Jan de Bont, Ellen Mirojnick, Ian Bryce, Kathleen Kennedy, Michael Crichton, Amblin Entertainment, Constant c Productions, Twister Productions, Universal Pictures, Warner Bros. Pictures, Joseph C. Nemec III, Anne-Marie Martin and Ken Nielsen

links.description: Also based on the true story of a natural disaster that had tragic consequences, The Perfect Storm (2000) broke ground with its VFX of the gigantic wave that eventually dooms the crew. Weta’s Christopher White worked on that sequence, along with the tornados from Twister (1996).

start.title: Twister
start.creator_credit: Jan de Bont, Ellen Mirojnick, Ian Bryce, Kathleen Kennedy, Michael Crichton, Amblin Entertainment, Constant c Productions, Twister Productions, Universal Pictures, Warner Bros. Pictures, Joseph C. Nemec III, Anne-Marie Martin and Ken Nielsen

end.title: Jurassic Park
end.creator_credit: Gerald R. Molen and Kathleen Kennedy
end.brief_description: An entrepreneur creates a theme park featuring living dinosaurs drawn from prehistoric DNA. Before opening day, he invites experts and his two grandchildren to experience the park and help calm anxious investors – a tough task when the dinosaurs escape.

constellations_other.name: Representing disasters
constellations_other.description: The devastating Indian Ocean tsunami was captured by many people on personal devices and home cameras, with the footage broadcast to the world. The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

constellations_other.name: Immersive sound: from THX to surround  sound
constellations_other.description: The THX sound became a symbol of immersive cinema, signalling pristine audio quality. From Return of the Jedi to videogames, creators explored spatial sound, dynamic mixing, and atmospheric audio, showing how sound shapes experience, emotion, and perception across multiple media forms.

links.description: Academy Award-nominee Christopher White brought the digital tornadoes in Twister to life by studying footage of real-life ones. Twister was written by Anne-Marie Martin and her husband Michael Crichton, who was also involved in the CGI-heavy classic, Jurassic Park.

start.title: Hereafter
start.creator_credit: Clint Eastwood, Kathleen Kennedy, Robert Lorenz, Amblin Entertainment, Malpaso Productions, The Kennedy/Marshall Company, James J. Murakami and Peter Morgan
start.brief_description: Three people — a blue-collar American, a French journalist and a London school boy — are touched by death in different ways.

constellations_other.name: Representing disasters

The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

end.title: The Impossible
end.creator_credit: J. A. Bayona, Anna Bingemann, Maria Reyes, Sparka Lee Hall, Álvaro Augustín, Belén Atienza, Ghislain Barrois, Apaches Entertainment, Canal+ España, Generalitat Valenciana, ICAA, Institut Valenciã de Cinematografia (IVAC), La Trini, Mediaset España, Summit Entertainment, Telecinco Cinema and Sergio G. Sánchez
end.headline_credit: Spain and United States of America, 2012
end.brief_description: In December 2004, close-knit family Maria, Henry and their three sons begin their winter vacation in Thailand only for the devastating Indian Ocean tsunami to strike.

constellations_other.name: Representing disasters
constellations_other.description: The devastating Indian Ocean tsunami was captured by many people on personal devices and home cameras, with the footage broadcast to the world. The event also inspired feature films that are part of a long tradition of disaster films, which often push the boundaries of VFX.

end.creator_credit_for_label: J. A. Bayona, Anna Bingemann, Maria Reyes, Sparka Lee Hall, Álvaro Augustín, Belén Atienza, Ghislain Barrois, Apaches Entertainment, Canal+ España, Generalitat Valenciana, ICAA, Institut Valenciã de Cinematografia (IVAC), La Trini, Mediaset España, Summit Entertainment, Telecinco Cinema and Sergio G. Sánchez

links.description: In Hereafter (2010), Cécile de France’s character plays a journalist whose life is forever altered after she survives the Indian Ocean tsunami on vacation. It was the first narrative film to feature the disaster, followed by The Impossible (2012).

start.title: The Impossible
start.creator_credit: J. A. Bayona, Anna Bingemann, Maria Reyes, Sparka Lee Hall, Álvaro Augustín, Belén Atienza, Ghislain Barrois, Apaches Entertainment, Canal+ España, Generalitat Valenciana, ICAA, Institut Valenciã de Cinematografia (IVAC), La Trini, Mediaset España, Summit Entertainment, Telecinco Cinema and Sergio G. links.description: The Impossible (2012) was based on the true story of the Álvarez family, who were separated and eventually survived the Indian Ocean tsunami. The technology needed to recreate the surges of waves on film was incredibly expensive, with wave VFX first perfected on The Perfect Storm (2000).

links.description: One of the deadliest natural disasters in living history, the Indian Ocean tsunami has been represented in pop culture through various books, television shows and films such as Clint Eastwood’s ensemble fantasy drama Hereafter (2010).
